opp.name: The Boston Beer Company_CLM_May2026
opp.ais_why:
* The engagement has stalled: the primary contact (Jonathan Young) was absent and the meeting was rescheduled, so the conversation never reached procurement requirements or solution discussion.
* No AI requirement has been established - discussion stayed at introductions and logistics ("reschedule the meeting once Jonathan becomes available").
* It is an early CLM opportunity (Merlin in scope) that has not progressed to a substantive discovery; the buyer has voiced no AI priority. Qualified at 10%.

sources: SOQL Q2/Q3 synthesis fields (unavailable or null)
Zero Avoma calls (opp+account discovery returned no matches)
Zero Salesforce tasks/events trailing 90d mentioning pain or ROI
metrics.narrative: No business problem, quantified impact, or ROI case has been articulated by the buyer across any source. Zero discovery calls conducted, zero Salesforce fields populated for Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, or Compelling_Event__c (fields not present in org or returned null). No tasks or events in the trailing 90 days reference pain points, savings targets, or efficiency gains. The deal remains in Qualified stage with no understanding of what drives the buyer's interest in CLM or Merlin AI. Without a quantified value case or even qualitative pain anchored to a named buyer, the deal cannot advance past discovery. The gap is KNOWLEDGE, not hygiene: we genuinely do not know what problem Boston Beer is solving.

sources: SOQL contact roles query (zero rows)
SOQL Q3 champion fields (unavailable or null)
Zero Avoma calls
LastActivityDate 07 Aug 2025, Jonathan Young unresponsive (inferred from task history)
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified, developed, or engaged across any source. Zero contact roles on the opportunity (SOQL). The single buyer contact attempted, Jonathan Young, has been unresponsive for 332 days (inferred from task history and LastActivityDate 07 Aug 2025), signaling deprioritization, internal handoff, or political shift. No Avoma calls to surface advocacy, internal sponsorship, or access to the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker. Salesforce fields X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c and Decision_Maker_Identified__c are not present or null. Zero evidence of a champion introducing us to other stakeholders, sharing internal docs, or pushing back on competitors. Without a champion, the deal has no internal advocate and no path to executive engagement or competitive insulation. The gap is KNOWLEDGE: champion-building requires active multi-threaded engagement, which has not occurred.

sources: SOQL Q3 competition fields (unavailable or null)
SF ground truth: Competitor unknown
Zero Avoma calls
Zero tasks/events mentioning competitors or RFP shortlists
competition.narrative: No competitors have been identified, mentioned, or benchmarked across any source. Salesforce fields Competitors__c and Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c are not present or null (SF ground truth: Competitor unknown). Zero Avoma calls means no buyer references to incumbent vendors, RFP shortlists, or alternative evaluations. Fields How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c (current stack), Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, and Why_not_Zycus__c are unavailable or null. Zero tasks or Next_Step__c entries mention competitive positioning. The absence of competition data may mean Boston Beer is sole-sourcing Zycus, or it may mean the deal is so early and so dark that competitive context has not emerged. For THIS deal, the lack of competition visibility is a KNOWLEDGE gap: we do not know whether we are competing, and if so against whom (Icertis, Aavenir, CLM incumbents, or build-vs-buy). Without competitive intelligence, we cannot tailor our Merlin AI wedge or iContract differentiation.

sources: SOQL Q3 pain fields (unavailable or null)
Zero Avoma calls
Zero tasks/events mentioning pain points or challenges
Product scope iContract+Merlin implies CLM/AI pain (inferred, not confirmed)
identify_pain.narrative: No operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer across any source. Zero discovery calls, zero Salesforce fields populated for Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Customer_Business_Problem__c, or Compelling_Event__c (fields unavailable or null). No tasks or events reference contract-management bottlenecks, compliance risk, supplier spend leakage, or manual workflows. The product scope (iContract CLM + Merlin AI) implies pain around contract lifecycle inefficiency and lack of AI-driven negotiation intelligence, but this is INFERRED from product fit, not confirmed by the buyer. Without a named pain owner or a quoted pain statement, the deal has no diagnosed problem to solve. The gap is KNOWLEDGE: pain discovery requires a live buyer conversation.

sources: SOQL Q3 paper-process fields (unavailable or null)
Zero Avoma calls
Zero tasks/events mentioning MSA, SOW, legal, or InfoSec
paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics, MSA/SOW requirements, legal review process, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, or implementation-scoping details have been surfaced across any source. Zero calls, zero tasks mentioning contracting or legal. Salesforce field Current_Contract_Expiration__c is not present or null (no forcing function from incumbent contract expiration). No SI or partner involvement documented. The deal is in Qualified stage with zero discovery, so paper-process discussion is premature, but the absence of any contracting or legal visibility means we cannot forecast the close timeline or identify bottlenecks. The gap is KNOWLEDGE: paper-process clarity requires advancement past discovery into commercials and legal kickoff, which cannot occur without re-engagement.

sources: SOQL contact roles query (zero rows)
SOQL Q3 EB/budget fields (unavailable or null)
Zero Avoma calls
Zero tasks/events mentioning finance or procurement executives
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified, engaged, or mapped across any source. Zero contact roles on the opportunity (SOQL returned empty set). No Avoma calls to surface budget authority or commercial decision-making. Salesforce fields X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, and Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c are not present in this org or returned null. No tasks or events reference a CFO, CPO, VP Finance, or senior procurement executive. For a $100K CLM+AI initiative, budget approval will require C-level or VP-level sponsorship, but zero executive engagement exists. The gap is KNOWLEDGE: we do not know who controls the commercial decision, and without discovery we have no pathway to identify them.

sources: SOQL Q3 process fields (unavailable or null)
Zero Avoma calls
Zero contact roles to infer committee structure
Zero tasks/events describing approval gates
decision_process.narrative: No decision process, approval chain, or buying-committee structure has been mapped across any source. Zero Avoma calls means no visibility into who joins at which stage, who escalates, or how Boston Beer runs vendor selection. Salesforce fields What_is_the_decision_process__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Multiple_approvals__c, and Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c are not present or null. Zero contact roles means no committee map. The single task attempt to Jonathan Young (unresponsive) provides no process insight. For an enterprise CLM deal, the typical process is discovery, demos, RFI/RFP, vendor shortlisting, commercials, and legal/InfoSec review over 12-15 months, but we do not know Boston Beer's actual motion, timeline, or gates. The gap is KNOWLEDGE: process discovery requires buyer engagement.

sources: SOQL Q3 decision-criteria fields (unavailable or null)
Zero Avoma calls (no use-case or RFP discussion)
Zero tasks/events mentioning evaluation or requirements
decision_criteria.narrative: No evaluation criteria, use-case priorities, or RFP/RFI requirements have been captured across any source. Zero discovery or demo calls conducted (Avoma). Salesforce fields Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, and AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c are not present in this org or returned null. No tasks or events reference test cases, proof points, or feature requirements. The buyer has not articulated what success looks like, which capabilities matter most (CLM workflow automation vs Merlin AI contract intelligence), or how they will score vendors. Without criteria, we cannot position iContract or Merlin effectively, and cannot anticipate competitive differentiation needs. The gap is KNOWLEDGE: criteria discovery requires a live buyer conversation, which has not occurred.

cro_panel.header: The Boston Beer Company — Qualified · $100K · closes 30 Dec

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 4 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 0 reflects a weak position: momentum 24 vs stage-expected 50. Offsetting it: momentum 24 vs stage-expected 50.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 24 (slipping backward): Engagement cadence dropped (pulse dark). Eased toward flat — 332d quiet (>30d window); momentum down 25pt (stalling).
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, commercial). Reduced because the most recent contact is stale (recency ×0.00).
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 16 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 1 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (0), nudged by commitment 16 and momentum 24, and dragged by risk 4. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

Last buyer activity 07 Aug 2025 (332 days ago, predates opp creation, likely a prior engagement or data artifact). Zero progression signals since creation: no stage changes, no amount changes, no next-step updates, no calls, no contact-role additions. Opportunity modified 18 Jun 2026 (17 days ago) but modification was administrative (no field-history evidence of buyer engagement or deal advancement). Deal is effectively dormant.

items.detail: Close date 30 Dec 2026 is 187 days out, but the deal is in Qualified stage with zero discovery completed and has been dark for 323 days. The enterprise CLM motion averages 12-15 months from discovery through close. Even with immediate restart, the close date is unachievable without compressing discovery, demos, RFI/RFP, vendor selection, commercials, contracting, and legal/InfoSec review into under 6 months, which is unrealistic for a $100K enterprise deal.

Zero Economic Buyer or C-level engagement. No senior procurement, finance, or supply chain executive has been contacted or linked to this opportunity. Without executive sponsorship, a $100K CLM+AI initiative will not secure budget approval or advance past discovery.

champion_strength.summary: No champion identified or developed. Jonathan Young, the single buyer contact attempted, has been unresponsive for 332 days. Zero evidence of advocacy, internal sponsorship, or access to Economic Buyer or Decision Maker. Deal has no internal advocate.

items.commitment: Jonathan Young must confirm availability and attend the rescheduled discovery call to enable the sales conversation to proceed

items.action: Executive connect: the deal owner's manager (Alexa Bradley) reaches out to Boston Beer senior procurement or finance executive (CFO, CPO, VP Supply Chain) via LinkedIn or warm intro to re-anchor the deal, confirm strategic fit for CLM+Merlin AI, and identify the current buying committee and Economic Buyer. Goal is to bypass the unresponsive single contact (Jonathan Young) and establish C-level sponsorship.
items.trigger: Deal dark for 332 days, single contact Jonathan Young unresponsive since 07 Aug 2025, zero Economic Buyer or executive engagement, zero contact roles, 178 days to close with zero discovery completed.
items.expected_effect: Re-engages deal at executive level, maps Economic Buyer and buying committee, creates pathway to discovery restart. Without this, deal remains frozen and should be close-lost.

items.action: Deal team launches multi-threaded outreach: identify 2-3 alternative Boston Beer stakeholders (procurement, legal/compliance, supply chain operations) via LinkedIn, ZoomInfo, or partner channel, and initiate parallel discovery conversations to map the buying committee, understand CLM pain points, and confirm project prioritization. Do not rely solely on Jonathan Young.
items.trigger: Single-threaded: only one buyer contact attempted (Jonathan Young), zero contact roles, zero buying committee mapped. If Jonathan remains disengaged, deal has no path forward.
items.expected_effect: Builds multi-threaded engagement, surfaces alternative champion or decision-maker, de-risks single-contact dependency. Enables discovery restart even if Jonathan Young remains unresponsive.

items.action: Deal team re-anchors close date to realistic timeline: if re-engagement succeeds, map Boston Beer's actual decision process (RFI/RFP gates, vendor selection timeline, legal/InfoSec review duration) and propose a new close date 12-15 months from discovery restart (Q2 or Q3 2027). If re-engagement fails by act_by date, recommend close-lost and reallocate pipeline capacity.
items.trigger: Close date 30 Dec 2026 is 178 days out, but deal is in Qualified stage with zero discovery completed and 332 days dark. Enterprise CLM motion averages 12-15 months; current close date is unachievable without compressing every stage into 6 months, which is unrealistic.
items.expected_effect: Aligns forecast and close date with realistic enterprise motion, prevents indefensible Pipeline forecast from polluting pipeline health. If close-lost, frees capacity for winnable deals.

items.action: Deal team runs discovery call (if re-engagement succeeds): map MEDDPICC end-to-end (Metrics: quantify CLM pain and ROI case; Economic Buyer: identify budget authority; Decision Criteria: capture RFP/evaluation priorities; Decision Process: map approval gates and timeline; Paper Process: understand contracting and InfoSec requirements; Identify Pain: diagnose contract-management bottlenecks; Champion: develop internal advocate with EB access). Position Merlin AI negotiation intelligence and I2O agentic workflow as differentiated value drivers.
items.trigger: Zero discovery completed: no buyer pain, business problem, or MEDDPICC elements captured across any source. Cannot advance to demos or RFI/RFP without understanding buyer problem, criteria, or decision process.
items.expected_effect: Captures MEDDPICC foundation required to position iContract+Merlin, tailor demos, and compete effectively. Converts dark deal into active qualified opportunity. Gate for all downstream milestones (demos, RFI/RFP, commercials).

items.action: Deal team secures a champion with Economic Buyer access: during discovery and multi-threaded outreach, identify a Boston Beer stakeholder (procurement lead, legal/compliance director, supply chain VP) who sees strategic value in CLM+AI, has access to the CFO or CPO, and is willing to advocate internally. Develop the champion by sharing ROI proof points, competitive differentiation (Merlin vs. Icertis/Aavenir), and helping them build the internal business case.
items.trigger: No champion identified or developed. Jonathan Young unresponsive for 332 days, zero advocacy signals, zero internal sponsorship. Without a champion, deal has no internal advocate and no path to executive engagement or competitive insulation.
items.expected_effect: Creates internal advocate who drives the buying process, introduces us to Economic Buyer and Decision Maker, and insulates us from competitive threats. Champion-building is continuous and gates commercial advancement.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 20 May 2026, qualified same day, close date 30 Dec 2026 (178 days out). Last verified buyer activity 07 Aug 2025 (332 days ago). Has been in Qualified stage for 46 days with zero progression signals. Enterprise CLM motion averages 12-15 months from discovery through contracting; compressing discovery, demos, RFI/RFP, vendor selection, commercials, legal, and InfoSec review into 178 days is unrealistic for a $100K deal. No time-in-stage data from submission dates (fields unavailable). Zero forward movement: no calls, no tasks, no contact roles added since creation. Deal is frozen.

north_star_verdict.headline: Dead deal: 332 days dark in Qualified stage, zero discovery completed, 178 days to close. Requires immediate executive escalation to re-engage or close-lost.

flags: RESTART OR DISQUALIFY WITHIN 7 DAYS: 323 days dark, zero discovery completed, 187 days to a Dec 2026 close that requires 12-15 months of enterprise motion. The deal owner must decide by 3 Jul 2026 whether to mount an executive-led restart (owner's manager Alexa Bradley engaging senior procurement leadership to validate need and secure Jonathan Young or an alternative champion) or disqualify and remove from pipeline. A third option (continue to drift) guarantees failure.

EXECUTIVE MULTI-THREAD IF RESTARTING: If the decision is to restart, the owner's manager Alexa Bradley should engage senior procurement leadership (the account has a Senior Executive Procurement Leader, Chief Supply Chain Officer, and SVP Supply Chain and Quality on record) to validate whether a CLM + Merlin initiative is active, who owns it, and whether Jonathan Young is the right contact or a different stakeholder should lead. An exec-to-exec conversation will uncover whether this opportunity is real or whether it should be closed lost.

BUILD CHAMPION FROM ZERO: Jonathan Young was a no-show 7 Aug 2025 and has not replied in 323 days, signaling disengagement or deprioritization. If he re-engages, assess his access to budget authority and advocacy immediately. If he remains unresponsive, identify an alternative champion among the senior procurement contacts and build advocacy from scratch. Without a champion by the second call, this deal will stall again.
COLD: No verified buyer engagement in 332 days (LastActivityDate 07 Aug 2025). Deal is frozen.
Single-threaded: Only one buyer contact attempted (Jonathan Young, unresponsive), zero contact roles, no buying committee mapped. If Jonathan remains disengaged, deal has no path forward.
Zero discovery: No calls, no pain identified, no MEDDPICC elements captured. Cannot advance to demos or RFI/RFP without understanding buyer problem, criteria, or decision process.
No Economic Buyer or executive engagement: Zero C-level or VP-level contact. $100K CLM+AI initiative requires budget authority; without EB, deal will stall at commercials.
No champion: Zero advocacy, zero internal sponsorship, zero access to decision-makers. Deal has no internal advocate.
Indefensible forecast: Pipeline forecast with 178 days to close, zero discovery, zero progression signals. Should be Omitted.

Close date unachievable: 30 Dec 2026 (178 days out) requires compressing 12-15 month enterprise motion into 6 months with zero discovery completed. Unrealistic without immediate executive escalation and buyer commitment.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal is catastrophically off-track: 332 days dark, zero discovery, zero multi-threading, zero executive engagement, zero MEDDPICC coverage, and 178 days to close. Immediate re-engagement or close-lost decision required.

Unknown whether Boston Beer is sole-sourcing Zycus or whether competitive context has not emerged due to zero discovery. Cannot assess competitive position or wedge without buyer engagement.

No misalignment signals, but also no alignment signals.

* No AI requirement has been established - discussion stayed at introductions and logistics ("reschedule the meeting once Jonathan becomes available").
* It is an early CLM opportunity (Merlin in scope) that has not progressed to a substantive discovery; the buyer has voiced no AI priority. Qualified at 10%.

deltas.subject: Close date unachievable: 30 Dec 2026 (178 days out) requires compressing 12-15 month enterprise motion into 6 months with zero discovery completed. Unrealistic without immediate executive escalation and buyer commitment.

Close date 30 Dec 2026 is 187 days out, but the deal is in Qualified stage with zero discovery completed and has been dark for 323 days. The enterprise CLM motion averages 12-15 months from discovery through close. Even with immediate restart, the close date is unachievable without compressing discovery, demos, RFI/RFP, vendor selection, commercials, contracting, and legal/InfoSec review into under 6 months, which is unrealistic for a $100K enterprise deal.

The intended primary contact Jonathan Young has been unresponsive for 323 days, signaling either deprioritization, internal political shift, or handoff to another stakeholder. Without a champion or an alternative engaged stakeholder, the deal has no internal advocate and will remain stalled.

RESTART OR DISQUALIFY WITHIN 7 DAYS: 323 days dark, zero discovery completed, 187 days to a Dec 2026 close that requires 12-15 months of enterprise motion. The deal owner must decide by 3 Jul 2026 whether to mount an executive-led restart (owner's manager Alexa Bradley engaging senior procurement leadership to validate need and secure Jonathan Young or an alternative champion) or disqualify and remove from pipeline. A third option (continue to drift) guarantees failure.

EXECUTIVE MULTI-THREAD IF RESTARTING: If the decision is to restart, the owner's manager Alexa Bradley should engage senior procurement leadership (the account has a Senior Executive Procurement Leader, Chief Supply Chain Officer, and SVP Supply Chain and Quality on record) to validate whether a CLM + Merlin initiative is active, who owns it, and whether Jonathan Young is the right contact or a different stakeholder should lead. An exec-to-exec conversation will uncover whether this opportunity is real or whether it should be closed lost.

BUILD CHAMPION FROM ZERO: Jonathan Young was a no-show 7 Aug 2025 and has not replied in 323 days, signaling disengagement or deprioritization. If he re-engages, assess his access to budget authority and advocacy immediately. If he remains unresponsive, identify an alternative champion among the senior procurement contacts and build advocacy from scratch. Without a champion by the second call, this deal will stall again.

gaps: Zero Avoma calls discovered via opp+account+attendee match (both get_all_meetings_for_opportunity and get_all_meetings_for_account returned zero transcribed buyer calls). Genuine knowledge gap: no call content exists to extract MEDDPICC, competitive intelligence, or buyer sentiment.

Salesforce synthesis-map fields (Q2/Q3 SOQL queries) returned INVALID_FIELD errors for most MEDDPICC and competition fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Competitors__c, X10a_Sponsor__c, Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c, etc.), indicating fields are not present in this Salesforce org or are named differently. Attempted retry with available fields; remaining fields unavailable. This limits Salesforce-based MEDDPICC synthesis but is NOT a knowledge gap when those facts also do not exist in calls or tasks (which is the case here).
Zero Salesforce contact roles on opportunity (SOQL returned empty set). Genuine knowledge gap: no buying committee, Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or Champion mapped.
Zero completed tasks or events in trailing 90 days (SOQL returned empty or minimal set with no discovery/demo content). Genuine knowledge gap: no recent buyer activity documented.
Next_Step__c field unavailable or null (SF ground truth). Hygiene gap: no next-step guidance populated, but this does not lower confidence when the deal is genuinely dark.

AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) unavailable or null (SOQL Q2). Cannot assess AI appetite via AIS scoring, but product scope includes Merlin AI. Knowledge gap: AI positioning strength unknown without discovery calls.
evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Trailing 365 days for field history, 90 days for tasks/events
